candidates.matchedPattern: URL validation regex with greedy wildcard (.+ or .*) — may be bypassable

findings.title: Command injection via filePath in git log execSync
findings.description: `getRecentCommitters` builds a shell command string by interpolating `absPath` (derived from `path.join(rootPath, filePath)`) into a shell template literal: ``git log --pretty=format:"%an\t%ae\t%aI" -n ${count} -- "${absPath}"``. The path is wrapped in double quotes only — no escaping. `filePath` originates from `glob` results in `packages/scanner/src/index.ts:158` (`relPath`) which preserves whatever filename exists in the user's project directory verbatim. On Linux/macOS, valid filenames can contain `"`, `` ` ``, `$`, `;`, `&`, `|`, `(`, `)`. A repo containing a file named e.g. `foo";touch /tmp/PWN;"bar.js` would cause `execSync` to spawn `sh -c 'git log ... -- "…/foo";touch /tmp/PWN;"bar.js"'`, executing the injected command. The scanner host is impacted; in normal local-run mode the developer's machine is fully exposed (read SSH keys, write authorized_keys, etc.). The 10s timeout does not mitigate — most exploitation completes in milliseconds. The project's own threat model in CLAUDE.md flags exactly this pattern: "`execSync` / `spawn` calls that interpolate `rootPath`, `filePath`, … especially in core/src/run.ts, processor/src/agents/codex-*".
findings.recommendation: Use the argv form of child_process: `spawnSync('git', ['log', '--pretty=format:%an\t%ae\t%aI', '-n', String(count), '--', absPath], { cwd: rootPath, … })`. Argv arrays bypass the shell entirely. Alternatively, validate that `filePath` matches `/^[A-Za-z0-9._/-]+$/` before use, or shell-escape via a vetted helper. The same fix applies to all execSync-with-template-literal sites in this file.

revalidation.reasoning: The reported vulnerable code is not present in the current file. I read packages/processor/src/enrich.ts fully and traced the relevant flow: enrichFileRecord passes record.filePath into getRecentCommitters, which derives absPath with path.join(rootPath, filePath). In the current implementation, getRecentCommitters invokes git with spawnSync('git', ['log', '--pretty=format:%an\t%ae\t%aI', '-n', String(count), '--', absPath], ...), so absPath is passed as a single argv element and is not parsed by a shell. Shell metacharacters in a filename such as quotes, semicolons, backticks, or dollar signs therefore remain literal path bytes from the child process perspective and cannot terminate the git command or inject another command. I also checked buildGitCommitterIndex, which similarly uses spawnSync with an argv array for the bulk git log path. The remaining execSync in this file, getRepoFromGitRemote, uses a static command string without interpolating filePath or rootPath into the shell command; rootPath is supplied as cwd. The scanner source still shows file paths originate from glob output and may preserve unusual names after separator normalization, but that no longer creates a shell injection sink in enrich.ts. I could not inspect git history because this sandbox has no .git directory, but the current source contains the recommended argv-form fix.
